title: Avoid using publicly writable directories
description: Operating systems have global directories where any user has write access. This rule raises an issue whenever it detects a hard-coded path to a publicly writable directory like /tmp or access to environment variables that point to publicly writable directories, e.g., TMP and TMPDIR.
recommendation: Avoid hard-coding paths to publicly writable directories or accessing environment variables that point to them.
impact: Exposure to race conditions, file manipulation, and potential security risks.

checks.message: Avoid hard-coding paths to publicly writable directories or accessing environment variables that point to them.
